intro: The clearest split is that Dub uses a freemium model and fd uses a free model. Dub is CI/CD for Applications tool for developers. Specializes in Link Management; fd is CI/CD for Applications tool for developers. Specializes in Fast Find. The data points Dub at automated build/test/deploy pipelines and team collaboration on code, and fd at automated build/test/deploy pipelines and team collaboration on code. Pricing: Dub is freemium, free tier; fd is free, free tier. Lock-in is low for Dub and low for fd, with DX at 5/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 5/5 vs 5/5. On listed strengths, Dub emphasises automated build/test/deploy pipelines and team collaboration on code, and fd emphasises automated build/test/deploy pipelines and team collaboration on code. Neither lists an enterprise tier, so contract-level buying may require a direct conversation. Honest trade-off: neither fits hobby projects, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: Dub is for mobile; fd is for mobile; decide based on which pricing model fits your budget.
verdict_a: Choose Dub if a freemium pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches mobile. Skip it if your context is hobby projects.
verdict_b: Choose fd if a free pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches mobile. Skip it if your context is hobby projects.

faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as unknown for Dub and unknown for fd. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Dub to fd?
faq.a: Dub is tagged with low lock-in (oss link management). fd is tagged with low lock-in (oss cli find replacement). Both sides are low lock-in, so a migration is feasible but still needs planning around data export and SDK rewrites.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Dub or fd?
faq.a: Both are rated 5/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is fd a good alternative to Dub?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches mobile, since both list the same fit. fd is flagged as not for hobby projects, so confirm your context does not fall there.
